summary: Fresh concrete tools, projects, design references, and civic-data games surfaced from Hacker News discussions.
telegram_footer: 10 items surfaced | 20/20 stories scraped | history + run artifact updated

items.name: Inkling
items.summary: An open-weights multimodal model with audio support for local experimentation.
items.why_included: A high-interest model release with practical local-run links and direct comparisons in the thread.
items.evidence: Commenters highlighted its audio capability and shared llama.cpp and GGUF paths for local use.

items.name: Samosa Chat
items.source_story_title: Running Gemma 4 26B at 5 tokens/sec on a 13-year-old Xeon with no GPU
items.summary: A local chat app for running open models on modest Mac hardware.
items.why_included: A commenter reported GPT-4-level local use on a 16GB MacBook Air.
items.evidence: The discussion linked Samosa Chat alongside a claimed 7–9 tokens/sec Qwen setup.

items.name: misa77
items.source_story_title: Show HN: misa77 - a codec that decodes 2x faster than LZ4 (at better ratios)
items.summary: An experimental compression codec optimized for very fast decompression.
items.why_included: Its measured throughput and clear engineering trade-off make it worth watching.
items.evidence: A Google compression engineer called the approach a known but useful speed-versus-encode-time trade-off.

items.name: Déjà Vu
items.source_story_title: Open-source memory for coding agents, synced over SSH
items.summary: Open-source persistent memory for coding agents, synchronized over SSH.
items.why_included: A focused, self-hostable approach to agent context persistence.
items.evidence: The Show HN submission presented the repository as agent memory that can sync between machines over SSH.

items.name: Telegram Serverless
items.summary: A serverless execution platform for Telegram bots and mini-app workflows.
items.why_included: It extends Telegram’s already broad bot platform with hosted execution.
items.evidence: Commenters called Telegram’s bot platform powerful while probing execution and storage limits.

items.name: clocks.dev
items.summary: A gallery of interactive digital clock designs and time-display experiments.
items.why_included: A compact, playful web collection that prompted concrete design discussion.
items.evidence: Commenters praised the collection and specifically recommended its timeline interaction.

items.name: ibiblio Icon Archive
items.summary: A browsable rescue archive of thousands of vintage web and file-format GIF icons.
items.why_included: It preserves a distinctive piece of early-web visual culture in a usable form.
items.evidence: Commenters appreciated the rescued source-extension icons and linked the public archive.

items.name: CityRider
items.source_story_title: The Conservationist Who Turned 40 Terabytes of Public Data into a Video Game
items.summary: An open-world Philadelphia game built from terrain, map, and civic data.
items.why_included: A creative use of public geospatial data with a live playable prototype.
items.evidence: A commenter described its Philadelphia terrain, tree inventory, litter-collection loop, and customization systems.

items.name: Siedler Österreich
items.source_story_title: The Conservationist Who Turned 40 Terabytes of Public Data into a Video Game
items.summary: A settlers-style game generated from Austrian public LiDAR and aerial data.
items.why_included: It is a concrete example of turning national open data into a playable world.
items.evidence: HN commenters supplied the direct game link after readers asked where to try it.

items.name: Teletun
items.summary: An experimental tunnel that carries network traffic over Telegram.
items.why_included: A pointed networking curiosity surfaced during the serverless-bot discussion.
items.evidence: A commenter linked Teletun while comparing Telegram tunneling with DNS tunneling.
